verdict.label: RED · Stand down / roll DTE longer
verdict.desc: The U.S.–Iran conflict is still live — overnight the U.S. launched fresh strikes on Iran (reportedly ~90 targets) and Tehran hit back at Gulf states, putting the fragile truce to the test after President Trump declared the ceasefire “over”. WTI is holding a two-session ~10% surge near $74. Gap risk stays elevated at 64/100 (WARM) and P(≥1% gap) is 42% — above the 35% trigger — so a GREEN verdict is locked out. Futures are mixed (Nasdaq bid on AI, Dow soft), but during an active, open-ended military conflict the overnight gap tail is unquantifiable and skewed down. Stand down on new credit spreads today, or push DTE out past the conflict window; this is not a premium-selling tape.

composite.band_note: WARM · down from 66 · conflict live but tape stabilizing

gap.heading: P(≥1% Overnight Gap to Fri Open)
gap.band_note: ABOVE the 35% danger line · GREEN locked out

focus.jbig: War still live · tape steadier · stand down
focus.jtag: Fresh U.S.–Iran strikes overnight, but futures stabilize as AI trade draws focus
focus.jnote: The environment is still hostile — just quieter this morning. Overnight the U.S. launched fresh airstrikes on Iran and Tehran retaliated by targeting U.S.-allied Gulf countries, testing the fragile truce after President Trump declared the ceasefire “over” and threatened to “hit them hard again.” Washington also revoked the waiver that let Iran sell crude. WTI is holding near $74 (Brent ~$78) after a two-session run of nearly 10%, and the 10-year yield popped to ~4.57–4.59% on inflation fear. Yet futures are mixed-to-firm — Nasdaq futures are up ~1% as investors refocus on the AI trade ahead of SK Hynix’s U.S. debut — and the VIX is easing slightly to ~16.9. Gold slipped a second day toward $4,030–4,100 as the dollar firmed. This is still a live, open-ended conflict — exactly the kind of headline tape a Sniper does not sell premium into.
focus.notdown: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) and Defense (LMT, RTX, NOC) remain the groups catching a bid — ConocoPhillips rose ~2% and Marathon Petroleum ~5% as oil supply fear lifts energy and conflict-demand lifts defense. But be honest: these are two-sided war trades, not clean Snipers. One de-escalation headline gaps them straight back down — Trump himself has said he doesn’t think the war fully restarts. Watch-only, not table-pounders.
focus.notup: Airlines & transports (DAL, UAL, AAL) and other fuel-sensitive cyclicals are pressured by the jet-fuel spike. But selling bear calls into an active-war, headline-driven tape carries violent-squeeze risk on any peace headline — and DAL reports Fri 7/10 pre-open, so any airline spread carries earnings risk too. The disciplined answer is to sell nothing new today.
focus.why: Why RED? Composite 64 is deep in WARM and P(≥1% gap) at 42% is well above the 35% trigger — either one alone locks out GREEN. More important than the numbers: an active military exchange with an oil shock is an unscheduled, open-ended tail — worse than a known event like CPI because you cannot time or size around the next headline, and fresh strikes just landed overnight. Stand down on new credit spreads, or if you must be positioned, push DTE out past the conflict window and cut size hard. Cash is a position today.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~16.9, easing slightly pre-market but still elevated off the strike headlines
Big events (next 1–3 days) — live war headlines, PEP reported (miss), DAL earnings Fri; CPI 7/14, PPI 7/15
World risk — active U.S.–Iran military exchange, fresh strikes overnight, Gulf bases threatened
Volatility trend — front-month VIX off its lows but pulling back this AM; curve near stress but not accelerating
Commodities — oil supply shock, +10% over 2 sessions on Hormuz risk; the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: highest since May · oil lifts inflation fear

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news and energy is the day’s clearest bid — ConocoPhillips and Marathon led yesterday. But this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation or ceasefire headline and crude gives back $5–7 in minutes, gapping your bull put the wrong way — and Trump has hinted the war may not fully restart. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a RED day, don’t open new premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above the open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Conflict-demand lifts the defense primes — Northrop and RTX have run hard over the past week — and structurally the story can continue. But the same peace-headline risk cuts the other way: a ceasefire announcement gaps this group down hard. Selling a bull put here is a bet the war continues — not a probability edge a Sniper wants to underwrite. Confirm no earnings inside DTE (LMT/RTX report late-Jul).
setups.check_note: Same logic as energy — the bid is a war premium. Stand aside; revisit only once the tape settles.

setups.read: Jet fuel just jumped with crude, a direct headwind for airlines — the fundamental lean is down, with consensus modeling a ~30% YoY EPS drop for DAL. But two problems: a peace headline squeezes shorts violently, and DAL reports Fri 7/10 pre-open (earnings inside any normal DTE). A bear call into a headline-driven tape with a print pending is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Earnings + war squeeze risk = no. Do not carry an airline spread through Friday’s DAL print.

setups.read: No clean qualifying post-earnings gap. PepsiCo reported a slight miss ($2.20 vs $2.21) and fell ~1.8% pre-market — a soft print inside a risk-off war tape, not a clean double-beat gap-up. Q2 season proper is just starting; DAL (Fri) is the next big print. Nothing qualifies into this tape.
setups.check_note: Nothing clean to validate — PEP miss doesn’t qualify as a Sniper Type A.

setups.read: Choppy, headline-driven tape with a live conflict — the exact environment where breakouts fail. With the Dow down over 500 points yesterday and fresh strikes overnight, no new clean high-volume ATH breakout is holding into this tape. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid environment; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline-driven whipsaw
scenarios.sub: Choppy; tape lurches on every Iran/oil headline, AI names try to hold the Nasdaq up, no clean trend

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation
scenarios.sub: Iran hits a Gulf base or Hormuz shipping is disrupted; oil runs toward $85–90, equities gap down

scenarios.label: Good case — De-escalation
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire/backchannel headline; oil gives back the spike, a relief bounce in stocks, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran retaliation — Tehran named 85 U.S. sites in Bahrain and Kuwait and struck back overnight after fresh U.S. strikes; any hit on personnel or bases gaps the whole tape instantly. This is the number-one thing to watch.

Strait of Hormuz shipping — roughly a fifth of the world’s oil moves through it, and recent attacks hit a Qatari LNG carrier and a Saudi tanker. Any closure sends crude (and jet fuel) vertical — the week’s biggest tail.

Oil & the 10-year — WTI near $74 after a ~10% two-day run feeds inflation fear; the 10Y hit ~4.57–4.59%, its highest since May, and September-hike odds jumped to ~70%. A rate pop compounds pressure on stocks.
DAL (Fri 7/10) — first airline print of the season, directly exposed to the fuel spike; consensus ~$1.44–1.50 EPS, down ~30% YoY. Single-stock gap risk stacked on headline risk.
CPI · Tue 7/14, PPI · Wed 7/15 — a hot inflation number on top of an oil shock, with the Fed’s June minutes flagging upside inflation risk, would be the worst-case macro combination.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is opening — PepsiCo reported this morning (slight miss, stock down ~1.8%) and Delta reports Fri 7/10, then the big banks land ~July 14–15 alongside CPI/PPI. With the market already on edge from the live Iran conflict, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Reported adj. EPS $2.20 vs $2.21 consensus — a one-cent miss — on revenue up 6.4% to $24.18B, and reiterated full-year guidance. Shares fell ~1.8% to ~$140 on softer-than-expected North America trends. A soft print, not a clean gap-up.
rows.angle: PEP already printed — no clean Type A here. A mild-miss fade in a risk-off tape isn’t a Sniper setup; move on.

rows.stock_sub: Delta · first airline of the season
rows.why: Consensus ~$1.44–1.50 EPS on ~$17.7B revenue — a ~30% YoY earnings drop. The Iran-driven fuel spike now cuts against airlines, pressuring margins and guidance, though DAL has beaten in each of the last four quarters. Directly in the crossfire of this week’s oil move.
rows.angle: Do not carry any airline spread through Friday. A miss-and-gap-down becomes a Type A bear-call setup next Monday.

rows.why: Real season opener — and they land the same days as CPI (7/14) and PPI (7/15), stacking macro and single-stock risk. Credit-sensitive names like JPMorgan sold off ~2.5% yesterday. Any bank spread must expire before its print.
rows.angle: Keep any financials spread to 7 days or fewer so it clears the earnings date — and mind the CPI/PPI overlap.

earnings.outro: Thu 7/9 – Fri 7/10: PepsiCo (reported) and Delta open the season; the rest is small-cap and off-cycle. It gets real next week: big banks ~Tue–Wed 7/14–15 alongside CPI/PPI, then the first mega-caps the week after — all against a live Iran backdrop.

rows.today: Mixed · Nasdaq futures bid on AI
